The Project
What is CampusConnect?

CampusConnect was developed as a two-month university project by a team of four students: Erlend Foss Møller, Vetle Amundsen, Wilhelm Ringstad, and Johannes B. Jacobsen.

The goal of the project was to address a real challenge at the University of Inland Norway (INN): information overload and difficult navigation across multiple university webpages. New students especially struggle to find clear answers about campus life, courses, deadlines, and services. We wanted to create a simpler, smarter solution.

CampusConnect is a Chrome extension that integrates directly with INN's website. It provides an AI-powered assistant that answers questions about university life, academic information, and student services — in both Norwegian and English.

Behind the extension is a custom-built backend deployed on Render, connected to a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline and the Llama 3.3-70B model via Groq. Instead of simply generating answers, our system searches through curated knowledge files about INN, retrieves the most relevant information, and then generates responses grounded in real university content.

The project evolved through three development phases:

  • 1A prototype extension scaffold to understand Chrome extension architecture
  • 2A local development version with direct API calls
  • 3A secure production backend with separated API handling, RAG retrieval, and cloud deployment

Security and reliability were central to our design. API keys are stored securely as environment variables on the backend, and the extension never exposes sensitive credentials.

CampusConnect was built entirely from scratch, including our retrieval system. It is designed to be scalable, modular, and adaptable to other universities in the future. What started as a school assignment became a functional AI assistant with real-world application potential.

What's Next
Future Work

Live Data Integration

Replace static knowledge files with periodic web crawling of official university pages to keep deadlines, schedules, and announcements continuously up to date. A hybrid crawl-and-cache strategy balances freshness with reliability.

Local LLM Hosting

Running an open-source model on dedicated infrastructure would increase data privacy, reduce reliance on third-party APIs, improve long-term cost predictability, and allow deeper customization of the language model.

API Fallback & Redundancy

Automatic failover to secondary providers or cached responses if the primary model is unavailable. This would significantly improve uptime and make CampusConnect production-grade reliable.

Feedback Loop

A structured rating system would let users flag inaccurate answers and surface common questions — enabling continuous improvement of retrieval quality, prompts, and knowledge coverage while preserving user privacy.

Semantic Search

Upgrading from TF-IDF to embedding-based vector search would dramatically improve the system's understanding of synonyms and natural language variations, making retrieval smarter and more context-aware.

Scalability Beyond INN

Because the knowledge base is modular and fully decoupled from the core system, the same architecture can be adapted for other universities, corporate portals, or public sector information services with minimal changes.
